subtitle: Regulated Delivery & Compliance-Embedded Agile Practice
hero_title:
Compliance built into
the sprint, not around it.
hero_sub: 15+ years embedding within stream-aligned Agile teams to deliver against business outcomes — with direct experience building compliance and risk-review checkpoints directly into sprint workflows in regulated environments, not bolting them on afterward.

the_case.answer: 15+ years of Scrum Master and Agile Coach roles across Eptura, Crocs, Understood.org, A&E, DigitasLBI/SunTrust, and more.
the_case.detail: Comfortably exceeds the stated threshold — Scrum Master and Agile delivery leadership has been the consistent throughline since 2014.

the_case.need: 3+ years in financial services (banking, lending, fintech, payments)
the_case.answer: Financial-services tenure is anchored at DigitasLBI, running Scrum within a SAFe-aligned, SOX-compliant program for SunTrust — and the same regulated-delivery discipline carried forward and sharpened at Crocs, under live audit review.
the_case.detail: My dedicated time inside the financial-services label runs under the three years this role names. What I've built since, and applied more rigorously with every engagement, is the underlying discipline this role actually needs exercised: compliance and risk review built directly into delivery, most recently and most demonstrably at Crocs under live audit review for a publicly traded company.

the_case.need: Regulated environments with compliance integrated into delivery
the_case.answer: Crocs: designed and embedded SOX-compliant Segregation of Duties (SOD) controls directly into Jira sprint workflow for a publicly traded retailer under active change-control review. DigitasLBI/SunTrust: SOX-compliant SAFe program.
the_case.detail: The SOD controls at Crocs weren't a separate compliance gate — they lived inside the sprint's definition of done, under live audit review. That's the harder, more current version of the same discipline this role needs.

the_case.answer: Daily practitioner across every recent engagement; built executive dashboards, workflow configurations, and SOD controls directly in Jira.
the_case.detail: Jira fluency at the configuration level, not just daily use — building compliance controls, dashboards, and workflow automation directly into the tool.

the_case.need: Demonstrated success coaching teams toward measurable behavioral improvement
the_case.answer: At Eptura, drove a 35%+ increase in delivery efficiency and a 20% reduction in sprint carryover across a ~10-team program through coaching and process standardization.
the_case.detail: Coaching that produces numbers a business can act on, not just a happier retro — measurable behavioral change at enterprise scale.

the_case.need: Active Scrum Master certification (SAFe Scrum Master or A-CSM preferred)
the_case.answer: Certified Scrum Master (CSM), Scrum Alliance — current. SAFe-aligned program experience at DigitasLBI/SunTrust and Eptura.
the_case.detail: Active CSM plus a decade of SAFe-aligned program experience across two enterprise engagements.

the_stack.headline: The compliance-embedded delivery toolkit.
the_stack.subhead: Tools used daily to keep delivery fast without creating audit exposure.

nodes.label: Jira & Compliance Controls
nodes.desc: Administration-level Jira fluency — SOD controls, workflow configuration, dashboards
nodes.pm_role: Designed and embedded SOX-compliant Segregation of Duties controls directly into Jira sprint workflow at Crocs, under live audit review.

nodes.label: Confluence & Smartsheet
nodes.pm_role: Used across every engagement for executive reporting and audit-ready documentation.

nodes.label: Azure DevOps
nodes.desc: Cloud delivery coordination for enterprise SaaS and retail environments
nodes.pm_role: Coordinated release readiness on Azure/Kubernetes infrastructure across multiple regulated engagements.

nodes.label: Claude Code & AI Tools
nodes.desc: Claude AI/Claude Code, ChatGPT, Jira AI, Microsoft Copilot
nodes.pm_role: Embedded Jira AI and Copilot directly into ceremonies at Crocs to cut administrative overhead ~30%.

nodes.label: GitHub Actions
nodes.pm_role: Used across recent projects for build-and-deploy automation.

when_things_break.scenario: A compliance or audit requirement threatens to slow delivery to a crawl
response: At Crocs, designed SOX-compliant Segregation of Duties controls
Embedded them directly into the Jira sprint workflow — not a separate compliance gate
Made risk review part of the definition of done, not an afterthought
Result: team stayed fast without creating an audit problem, under live change-control review

when_things_break.scenario: A team's sprint commitments keep slipping and no one has diagnosed why
response: At Eptura, coached ~10 distributed teams on backlog readiness and WIP discipline
Used dashboard data to diagnose the real pattern, not just react to the latest miss
Drove root cause analysis over guesswork
Result: 35%+ increase in delivery efficiency, 20% reduction in sprint carryover

when_things_break.scenario: A distributed, multi-country team needs a Scrum Master who can stabilize a program in crisis
response: At Understood.org, inherited a distressed, multi-year program spanning 12+ countries
Took over in its final crucial months, under real pressure
Stabilized ceremony cadence and reporting transparency immediately
Result: delivered the replatform through QA/UAT with zero critical regressions

how_i_run_a_room: Compliance and risk-review steps get built into the sprint definition of done, not bolted on as a separate gate
Every ceremony protects team focus from outside disruption — impediments get removed, not just logged
RAID log maintained and reviewed at every stakeholder touchpoint; risks named before they become issues
Retrospectives produce measurable, tangible process changes — not action lists that expire
Pushes back on unrealistic scope or timeline commitments directly, with data to back it up
Clear line between Scrum Master and Product Owner responsibilities — coaching both roles to stay in their lane

points: Designed and embedded SOX-compliant Segregation of Duties controls in Jira sprint workflow
Built 'Release Radar,' a real-time release-readiness tracking framework
Reduced administrative overhead ~30% via Jira AI and Copilot
Facilitated ceremonies across Engineering, QA, Change Management, and Business
Coached ~10 distributed teams on backlog readiness and WIP discipline
Built Jira dashboards tracking velocity, sprint health, and cumulative flow
Drove 35%+ increase in delivery efficiency, 20% reduction in sprint carryover
Produced executive dashboards for senior leadership
Stabilized a distressed, multi-year program spanning 12+ countries
Steered a Sitecore-to-Contentful replatform to on-time launch
Primary point of contact for the VP of Engineering
Delivered through QA/UAT with zero critical release regressions
Scrum Master within a SAFe enterprise program for a financial-services client

before: Compliance and risk review feel like a separate track bolted onto delivery
Engineering and audit/compliance stakeholders work in friction, not partnership
Teams slow down every time a compliance question surfaces
Risk review happens after the sprint, when it's too late to change course
after: Risk review embedded directly into the sprint cycle
A team that moves fast without creating audit exposure
A Scrum Master who speaks both delivery and compliance fluently
Compliance becomes part of the definition of done, not a separate gate

the_ramp.deliverable: Review current sprint cadence, backlog health, and existing compliance/risk-review checkpoints for the assigned stream-aligned team(s); read OKR documentation to understand what business outcomes delivery is tied to.
Own ceremony facilitation for assigned teams; identify where compliance/risk review can be embedded more cleanly into the sprint cycle.
Establish working relationships with Product Owners and engineering leads; deliver the first retrospective-driven process improvement.
